description:
WHY: agents and humans get confused when a repo's docs reference bl but the repo isn't actually bl-init'ed (or vice versa, or state has drifted). Today these only surface as opaque errors mid-workflow. A diagnostic command that flags drift up front turns "bl prime mysteriously fails" into "AGENTS.md mentions bl but .balls/ is missing — either run bl init or remove the references."

CHECKS to consider (start small, expand as cases emerge):
  - AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / README mentions bl but no .balls/
  - .balls/ present but state branch missing or far behind origin
  - .balls/worktree exists but isn't a valid linked worktree
  - Stale claims (claim files for tasks that no longer exist or are already closed)
  - Worktrees in .balls-worktrees/ with no matching task or claim
  - Override file .balls/local/tasks_dir points somewhere that doesn't exist
  - Bare repo on a bl version that doesn't support bare discovery (once that gap is closed)

NON-GOALS: not bl repair (which already exists and mutates). doctor is read-only — it diagnoses, suggests, never touches state. Keep "repair" as the only action verb.

PRIORITY: lower than the bare-repo discovery bug and the error-message work — those will eliminate the cases doctor would otherwise carry. Build doctor once those land and we can see what's actually left over. Source: external user feedback noting that an AGENTS.md referencing bl in a not-init'd repo had no easy way to be detected.
title: bl doctor: read-only diagnostic for repo/bl state drift
